title_guess: Duns Tew, Hinton Parva, Ashdown House, Stonehenge & Bemerton (Eve's birthday)

pages.text:
looked around + Simon liked a round table with laminated wood showing by Jason Heap who lives on the Isle of Wight. He met him. I went back to the van + read. We had lunch + set off north, stopping for tea on the way. We got home + Simon mowed the lawn before the church Book Club came.

Friday June 6th.
   3.10. Simon, Eve + Tinder set off for Charles'. 2 flats at [?Suns] Tew. There we arrived just before William + Bryony, Joseph + Seb. Then John came. Finally Charles, who later collected Jenny. Jenny had cooked a delicious big lasagna + salad + W + B mortgaged the house + brought me a yummy cake from Conditor + Cooke. I had lovely presents. Sensible people fell into bed + Charles + Jenny + John went off to the pub. Poor Charles had had an exam that day.
June 7th. I was fast asleep + Simon shaving when Bryony arrived at 8 with the Z boys

They were very patient while John (who was leaving early to go back to Swansea) climbed into bed with us & we had tea, dressed & had breakfast. We & joined us for breakfast. It was so nice to have a meeting place for visitors.
        They left. Charles & Jenny got up. Jenny was fed up at having another driving lesson. We chatted. We left for Conta's house at Hinton Parva. We looked at the church next door. St. Swithins. Conta had paid £500 to prevent concrete being put over all the stones by the new warden.
        Iona & Theo were studying for their second year medical exams. Really too nice a day to study. Conta was annoyed that Wanda's gymkana was cancelled because lightning had been forecast. Wanda is at C.V.L.C. Mike was in Wales golfing with a friend. Tinder had a wild run around with Conta's dogs. Iona wonders if he could breed with Willow.

captions: ENGLISH HERITAGE STONEHENGE — Step into England's story
pages.text:
We got in for free with EH/NT membership. Otherwise— family £39

[glued-in Stonehenge English Heritage ticket: "ENGLISH HERITAGE STONEHENGE / Step into England's story / Join English Heritage FOR MORE GREAT DAYS OUT / Web Member Adult / Tour Date: 08 Jun 2014 09.00 / Valid on day of purchase only"]

        On to __Ashdown House__, a hunting lodge built in 1662 by Lord Craven (nouveau riche Cavalier) for his girl-friend Elizabeth, daughter of James I, sister of Charles I & wife of King of Bohemia? who had lost his throne. She loved hunting. You could view the land for miles around at the top of the house, cupola balcony.

The house is tenanted. It is tidy with 3 reception rooms + 8 bedrooms all closed. The 3.15. tour took us up the staircase, very wide, past paintings given to Craven by Elizabeth, 10 of her children. Excellent guide called Nicola. There's a long, underground passage out from the cellar because of 'nervousness' after the Civil War. The avenues are a mile long.
    We camped at a really nice site, Orcheston village, Stonehenge Touring Campsite* [?★★★] very welcoming, clean, friendly, library. There was a trailer serving grilled food, so we had chips with our chicken salad.
Sunday
9.0 We had first-entry tickets for Stonehenge. It was just as well because when we came to leave (10.30.) it was really crowded. The new visitors' centre, opened in the last 12 months, is very good. Light + airy. The exhibits capture the scale of Stonehenge. The big stones are sareen stones, dragged

captions: George Herbert, West Front, Salisbury Cath[edral]
pages.text:
19 miles from Marlborough. The smaller, blue stones came 150 miles by water + land, from Prestelli in Wales.

[photo: stone statue on cathedral facade]

George Herbert, West Front, Salisbury Cath[edral]

captions: Bemerton Church Window - Ferrar and Herbert
pages.text:
On to Bemerton. We are reading "Music at Midnight by Drury, about George Herbert, for the church book club. The village has huge water meadows, which used to be flooded every winter to keep them fertile. It felt abit like Amiens. We met an old lady who was friendly & told us about the George Herbert festival July 11". (John's graduation.)
    On to the campsite.... which couldn't have us. A farm campsite *
    As dusk fell there was a raucous noise from the oak tree next to us. Screeching, murder, mayhem. It went on for ages Then 2 owls flew swooping away. When they came back there was more noise. It went on & on thru the night as they fed

their young.
    Finally Simon got an answer on the campsite telephone. The husband. He said we shd put £15 in the box. Simon said he'd see if he had the change. It seemed grossly expensive; all we had was mown grass & owls. So we left £10.
    On to Farnham where Simon dropped me off & went on to a Canna meeting with Richard. I shopped & went to see Jane's new house. It's lovely & light. She watches the TV, Wimbledon, from behind a silk parasol because of the sun's glare.
    We met up with Mary & Richard & Hattie & Piers for a good pub lunch. Simon drove home with Tinder, after leaving me at the station to visit Kate at Woking & then William at Waterloo.
